source_document: The Medical Devices (Post-market Surveillance Requirements) (Amendment) (Great Britain) Regulations 2024 (SI 2024/1368), Part 4A (Regs 44ZC-44ZR)

rules.obligation: Manufacturer must maintain a PMS system proportionate to risk and appropriate to device type, with ongoing data analysis throughout the device lifetime.
rules.agent_instruction: When UK market is present, confirm the PMS system is proportionate to device risk and operates throughout the device lifetime. Reference data flowing through it without narrating its existence.

rules.obligation: PMS plan must specify device lifetime, processes for collecting/assessing incidents, complaints, feedback and trend data, statistical methodology, and threshold values for risk reassessment.
rules.agent_instruction: Reference the PMS plan when describing trend statistical methodology and threshold values used for trend reporting under Reg 44ZN. PMS plan must be available within 3 working days on request.

rules.obligation: On identifying a risk or suspected non-conformity, manufacturer must take necessary preventive or corrective action as soon as possible and notify the UK Responsible Person, approved body and Secretary of State (for FSCAs).
rules.agent_instruction: Section I must show that any UK-relevant CAPAs or FSCAs were notified to UK RP, approved body, and Secretary of State.

rules.obligation: Serious incidents must be reported to the Secretary of State (via MHRA) within 15 days of awareness; deaths or unanticipated serious deteriorations within 10 days; serious public-health threats within 2 days.
rules.agent_instruction: Section D must report each UK serious incident with the reporting timeline observed. Include manufacturer/UK RP details, device with UDIs, incident description, preliminary conclusions, and FSCA consideration.

rules.obligation: Manufacturer must investigate and submit a final report with methods/conclusions, FSCA details, and details of similar incidents involving the same device model. Devices/batches must not be altered before informing the Secretary of State.
rules.agent_instruction: Document final-report conclusions for each UK serious incident; reference any similar incidents on the same model. Periodic summary reports may replace individual reports for similar incidents by agreement.

rules.obligation: Before taking an FSCA, manufacturer must produce a risk assessment, submit an initial report and proposed Field Safety Notice (FSN) to the Secretary of State; FSN must identify devices with UDIs in searchable format. After FSCA, submit a final report with outcome and effectiveness evidence.
rules.agent_instruction: Section H must list each UK FSCA with FSN reference, devices identified by UDI, action taken by users, and post-FSCA effectiveness evidence.

rules.obligation: FSCAs taken outside GB on the same device model must be reported to the Secretary of State if the same FSCA is not being taken in GB, with justification.
rules.agent_instruction: If any FSCA was taken outside GB on the same device model and not replicated in GB, document the justification.

rules.obligation: For Class I (and IVD Class A-B) a Post-Market Surveillance Report (PMSR) is required instead of a PSUR; produced within 3 years and updated every 3 years.
rules.agent_instruction: For Class I devices on the UK market, state that the regulatory obligation is a PMSR (Reg 44ZL) on a 3-year cycle, and this PSUR-format document is voluntary.

rules.obligation: PSUR required: annually for Class IIb / III / implantable, biennially for Class IIa. Must be submitted to the approved body; for Class III/implantable, the approved body must review and issue a report to manufacturer and UK RP.
rules.agent_instruction: Confirm the cadence and submission target match the device's UK MDR class.

rules.obligation: PSUR must report number of devices placed on the UK market, devices put into service in the UK without being placed on the market, characteristics of the UK user population, estimated UK and worldwide population sizes, and usage frequency where practicable.
rules.agent_instruction: Section C Table 1 must include a UK row sourced from statistics.uk_units (pre-computed). Do NOT merge UK with EEA or Rest of World. Reference the UK population estimate explicitly.

rules.obligation: Manufacturer must report significant increases in incident frequency or severity that could adversely impact the required risk analysis. Significance is determined by the PMS plan statistical methodology. For IVDs, also report significant increases in expected erroneous results. Investigate and submit a final report with conclusions and actions.
rules.agent_instruction: Section G trend assessment must explicitly include UK-specific data when available, with conclusions and actions for any reportable trend.

rules.obligation: Retain all PMS documentation for the longer of: the PMS period for the device model, OR 15 years for implantable devices / 10 years for all other devices. Both manufacturer and UK Responsible Person must retain.
rules.agent_instruction: Section B may state the retention commitment when documenting PMS arrangements.

rules.obligation: Documentation must be provided to the Secretary of State within 3 working days of request; the Secretary of State may extend timelines if reasonable.
rules.agent_instruction: Cover-page or Section B may confirm the 3-working-day availability commitment.

rules.obligation: UK Responsible Person details must come from the source device context only.
rules.agent_instruction: Use ONLY uk_responsible_person from device_context. If absent, write 'UK Responsible Person details not available in source data.' Do NOT invent UK RP names, addresses or registration numbers.
